pages.full_text:
__Nov 3rd__
	We set off to Lymington & wandered around the town looking at market stalls, seeing the quayside too. We had elevenses & then drove on to Hattie's.
	We had lunch with Hattie + Simon drove into Southampton with James, while __Hattie__ + I walked the younger children to the park. Chandlers Ford
	The girls gave John + Charles marvellous baths. Edward had spent £7 of his QE II left-over money on fireworks, + John + Charles stood at the window to watch them all. We all enjoyed them.
	On the Sunday we went to St Martin's church. Very good sermon
pages.notes: Year not stated on page; volume covers 1989-2003. 'QE II' likely refers to leftover spending money from a QE2 cruise trip. 'Hattie' appears underlined (a person's name, not a place). The word 'Chandlers Ford' appears at the right margin of a line — may be a marginal annotation indicating the park location near Southampton. St Martin's church not further identified.

pages.full_text:
from lay-reader Pauline Lewis.
	We went on to pick up Thomas at lunch at __Jane + Harry's__. Katie Ireland was there too.
	Richard + Mary brought Thomas there. John called Holly "Lollipop" on the way home.
	We stopped in the Cotswolds to pick up Simon's 'cello.
	It has been a fabulous year's camping.
pages.notes: This page is a direct continuation of the left page (Nov 3rd entry). 'Jane + Harry's' is underlined — a person's home rather than a place name. The closing line 'It has been a fabulous year's camping.' with an underline decoration suggests this is the end-of-year summary for the diary volume's camping season. No year is explicitly given.
